message:
fix(distributed/recovery): do not trust collector verified flag when verify_integrity is enabled

RecoveryOrchestrator::reconstruct_from_collected() was using the
cs.verified flag from CollectedSymbol to decide whether to construct
an AuthenticatedSymbol as pre-verified (skipping integrity checks) or
unverified (requiring pipeline re-verification). When the decoder's
verify_integrity config is true, trusting this flag is a security gap:
the collector may be an untrusted peer, or the verified flag may
reflect a check from a different security context (different keys,
different epoch).

Now introduces trust_verified_flag = !self.decoder.config.verify_integrity.
When verify_integrity is enabled, all symbols are forced through
AuthenticatedSymbol::from_parts() regardless of cs.verified, ensuring
the pipeline always performs its own integrity check. When verify_integrity
is disabled (trusted environments), the collector's flag is still honored
for performance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
